mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals weakening labor demand that precedes consumer spending declines and profit compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when the trigger fires during any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and residential construction (HOUST) signals broad-based demand acceleration that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the joint signal is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform the best single-series z-score in predicting 20-day SPX direction over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2%, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal an overheating economy that invites additional monetary restraint, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 2% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals an unexpected monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive following FEDFUNDS drops > 0.25pp (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and moderation in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a goldilocks scenario of strong hiring without overheating, lifting profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling CPIAUCSL does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by an unemployment rate below 4%, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals diminishing slack and impending Fed tightening that outweighs top-line growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly negative when UNRATE < 4% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and falling operating leverage that drags corporate earnings expectations lower, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative following month-over-month INDPRO declines (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
